_note: Ground truth for the Ema Software Engineering Lead, Machine Learning JD (swe_jd_ema.txt). Annotated directly from JD text — required skills derived from 'You will:' responsibilities (implied non-negotiable) and core 'Ideally you'd have:' technical competencies. Preferred skills are the softer/nice-to-have items from the same section.

_annotation_notes.framing: The JD uses 'Ideally, you'd have:' for all requirements — but this is a Lead ML role at a funded startup. Skills like Python, PyTorch/TensorFlow, NLP, LLMs, SQL, and MLOps are non-negotiable in practice regardless of the softened framing.
_annotation_notes.pytorch_vs_tensorflow: JD says 'TensorFlow or PyTorch' — both are listed as required. A candidate with only one should still match via the LLM skill matcher (either satisfies the requirement).
_annotation_notes.retrieval_ranking_rl_agents: Listed under 'deep understanding of ANY of...' in the JD — these are required as a group but the candidate only needs depth in at least one. Listed individually here so the matcher can assess coverage across the group.
_annotation_notes.education: Master's or PhD listed as preferred — not added as a required_skill since it's framed as 'ideally' and the JD doesn't gate on it explicitly.
_annotation_notes.soft_skills: Problem-solving, attention to detail, startup environment fit — excluded from both lists. Not assessable from resume text alone.
_annotation_notes.preferred_framing: GCP and Azure are listed as 'familiarity with cloud platforms like GCP or Azure' — cloud platforms is required (as general), specific providers are preferred.
